source.zh: 《往年纪事》（约1113年）；《伊戈尔远征记》（12世纪末）；赫尔莫尔德《斯拉夫编年史》（约1170年）；《伊帕季耶夫编年史》1114年条所引马拉拉斯编年史斯拉夫译本；阿法纳西耶夫《俄罗斯民间传说集》（1859）、《斯拉夫人对自然的诗意观照》（1865—1869）；丘宾斯基《西俄地区民族志统计考察队纪要》第一卷（1872）；戈洛瓦茨基《加利西亚与匈牙利罗斯民歌集》（1878）；别索诺夫《行乞歌者集》（1861—1864）所收《鸽书》灵歌；丹哈特《自然传说集》第一卷（1907）
source.en: Primary Chronicle (Povest' vremennykh let, c. 1113); The Tale of Igor's Campaign (late 12th c.); Helmold of Bosau, Chronica Slavorum (c. 1170); the Slavonic Malalas passage in the Hypatian Chronicle, entry for 1114; A. N. Afanasyev, Narodnye russkie legendy (1859) and Poeticheskie vozzreniya slavyan na prirodu (1865-69); P. P. Chubynsky, Trudy etnografichesko-statisticheskoi ekspeditsii v Zapadno-Russkii krai, vol. 1 (1872); Ya. Holovatsky, Narodnye pesni Galitskoi i Ugorskoi Rusi (1878); the Golubinaya kniga verse in P. Bezsonov, Kaliki perekhozhie (1861-64); Oskar Dähnhardt, Natursagen, vol. 1 (1907)

note.zh:
前基督教时代的斯拉夫人没有留下任何创世叙事的一手记录。中世纪文献只给神名与祭仪，不给故事：《往年纪事》记980年弗拉基米尔在基辅山上立起佩伦（木身、银头、金须）与霍尔斯、达日博格、斯特里博格、西马尔格尔、莫科什的偶像，又记988年佩伦被拖到第聂伯河、用棍棒抽打后放走；《伊戈尔远征记》称罗斯人为「达日博格的子孙」、风为「斯特里博格的子孙」、歌手博扬为「韦列斯之孙」；赫尔莫尔德只提到波罗的海斯拉夫人诅咒时呼「切尔诺博格，即黑神」——与之对称的「别洛博格」在中世纪文献里根本不存在，是后人补出来的。《伊帕季耶夫编年史》1114年条把斯瓦罗格等同于赫菲斯托斯、达日博格等同于赫利俄斯，可那段本是拜占庭马拉拉斯编年史讲埃及诸王的文字，斯拉夫译者顺手换了名字。这些没有一条是创世神话。

下面这条线依据的是19世纪在俄罗斯、乌克兰、白俄罗斯与保加利亚乡村记录下来的「潜水取土」传说和灵歌。它们晚出，且已彻底基督教化：动手的是神与撒旦，不是佩伦或斯瓦罗格；异教诸神在现存的创世传说里一次也没出现。这套二元格局与鲍格米勒派异端的关系是一桩老争论——一派认为是异端神学下渗进了民间故事，另一派指出「潜水取土」是从芬兰-乌戈尔、突厥、西伯利亚一直分布到北美的古老类型，基督教二元论只是后来罩上去的一层皮。两说至今没有定论。

各版本互相打架，正文里如实并列，不做抹平：喀尔巴阡颂歌里潜水的是两只鸽子（另一些记录里是三只，海心的树也从两棵橡树变成一棵青枫），散文传说里是撒旦；山的来历有两说，一说是撒旦吐出的偷藏之土，一说是神把摊得过大的地捏出褶子；教神办法的，有的记录是刺猬，有的是魔鬼自言自语被蜜蜂偷听。这些片段采自不同地区、不同体裁，串成一条时间线是编者的排法，没有哪个村子从头到尾这么讲。另有三处需要提防：把「罗德」当至高创世神是雷巴科夫1981年的重构，中世纪训诫文只说人们向罗德与产神献祭，没说他造过世界；佩伦与韦列斯的所谓「基本神话」是伊万诺夫与托波罗夫的构拟，不是记录下来的文本；《韦列斯之书》是20世纪伪作，学界一致否定，而今天网上流传的「斯拉夫创世神话」大半出自那里。
note.en:
Nothing first-hand survives from the pre-Christian Slavs about how the world began. The medieval sources give names and rites, never narrative. The Primary Chronicle records that in 980 Vladimir set up idols on the hill at Kiev, Perun carved of wood with a silver head and a gold moustache among them, along with Khors, Dazhbog, Stribog, Simargl and Mokosh, and that in 988 Perun was dragged to the Dnieper, beaten with sticks and floated downstream. The Tale of Igor's Campaign calls the Rus the grandsons of Dazhbog, the winds the grandsons of Stribog, and the singer Boyan the grandson of Veles. Helmold of Bosau reports only that the Baltic Slavs cursed by the name of Zcerneboch, the black god; the symmetrical Belobog has no medieval attestation whatever and was supplied by later writers. The Hypatian Chronicle's entry for 1114 equates Svarog with Hephaistos and Dazhbog with Helios, but that passage is a Byzantine chronicle about the kings of Egypt with Slavic names dropped in by the translator. None of it is a cosmogony.

The sequence below rests instead on earth-diver legends and spiritual verses taken down in Russian, Ukrainian, Belarusian and Bulgarian villages in the nineteenth century. They are late and thoroughly Christianised: the work is done by God and Satan, and the pagan gods appear in the surviving creation stories not once. Whether the dualism descends from the Bogomil heresy is a long-running argument. One side reads the legends as heretical theology seeping into folklore; the other points out that the earth-diver is an old and very widely spread type, found among Finno-Ugric, Turkic and Siberian peoples and across North America, with the Christian dualist casting laid over the top. Neither case is settled.

The recorded versions contradict each other, and the stages keep the contradictions instead of smoothing them: the diver is a pair of doves in the Carpathian carol, three doves in other recordings of it, where the two oaks in the sea become a single green sycamore, and Satan in the prose legends; the mountains come either from the earth Satan hid in his mouth or from God crumpling an oversized earth to fit the sky; the advice about the sky is given by a hedgehog in some recordings and overheard from the devil in others. The pieces were collected in different regions and different genres, and stringing them into one line is an editorial act, not the way any one village told it. Three further cautions: Rod as a supreme creator god is Boris Rybakov's reconstruction of 1981, and the medieval sermons say only that people made offerings to Rod and the rozhanitsy, never that he made the world; the 'basic myth' of Perun against Veles is a reconstruction by Ivanov and Toporov, not a recorded text; and the Book of Veles, from which most of the Slavic creation material circulating online derives, is a twentieth-century forgery.

title.en: No sky, no earth, only water

text.zh: 所有记录下来的斯拉夫创世传说都从同一句话起头：那时没有天，也没有地，只有一片水。加利西亚的一首圣诞颂歌唱道，世界还没有开头的时候，只有蓝色的海，海心里立着两棵橡树。基督教化的散文传说则说，神在这片水上走动，水面浮起一团泡沫。没有一则记录交代这片水是从哪儿来的。
text.en: Every recorded Slavic account of the beginning opens the same way: there was no sky and no earth, only water. A Christmas carol from Galicia sings that before the world had any beginning there was only the blue sea, with two oak trees standing in the middle of it. The prose legends, all of them told after conversion, have God walking on that water while a scum of foam gathers on its surface. Not one version says where the water came from.

title.en: Two doves, or God and Satan

text.zh: 颂歌里，两只鸽子落在海心那两棵橡树上，彼此商量着潜到海底去。散文传说里，水上并肩站着的是神与撒旦：有的记录说撒旦是从那团泡沫里钻出来的，有的说神先看见的是一只浮在水面的水鸟，那就是他。两套版本都不交代这两个存在的来历，也都没有第三者在场。
text.en: In the carol two doves alight on the oaks in the middle of the sea and take counsel with one another about diving to the bottom. In the prose legends the pair on the water is God and Satan: in some recordings Satan climbs out of the floating foam, in others the first thing God sees is a waterbird riding the swell, and the bird is Satan. Neither type accounts for where the two came from, and neither brings in a third.

title.zh: 从海底捞上来的一把沙
title.en: A fistful of sand off the sea floor

text.zh: 神打发撒旦潜下去取土。多数俄语和乌克兰语的记录说他潜了三次：头两次他捞土时说「这是我的」，土便在他手里被水冲得精光；第三次他照吩咐说「以神的名取」，才攥着一把沙回到水面。神把沙撒在水上，沙一沾水就往四面长开，长成干地。颂歌里没有撒旦：两只鸽子一同潜下去，带回细沙和一块金石头，细沙撒出黑土、凉水和青草。
text.en: God sends Satan down for earth. Most Russian and Ukrainian recordings have him dive three times: twice he grabs the mud in his own name and the water strips it out of his fists on the way up, and only on the third dive, when he takes it in God's name as he was told, does a fistful of sand come up with him. God scatters the sand over the water and it spreads until it is dry land. The carol has no Satan at all: the two doves go down together and bring back fine sand and a golden stone, and the sand sown on the water becomes black earth, cold water and green grass.

text.zh: 撒旦上来时腮帮里藏了几粒沙，打算给自己也留一块地。神撒下的沙在水上疯长，他嘴里那几粒也跟着长，涨得他喘不上气，只好一路咳吐出去——落下的成了山岭、悬崖、乱石和沼泽。大地从此不平。另一路传说讲，神造完地就躺在地上睡着了，撒旦要把他拖下水淹死，往东拖、往西拖、往北拖、往南拖，可他每走一程，地就朝那个方向再长出一截，怎么也拖不到水边。
text.en: Satan surfaced with a few grains hidden in his cheek, meaning to keep a patch of land for himself. When God's sand began to swell on the water, the grains in his mouth swelled too, until they were choking him and he had to cough them out as he went; where they fell they became mountains, cliffs, scree and bog. That is why the earth is not level. Another line of legend has God asleep on the new land and Satan hauling him toward the sea to drown him, east, then west, then north, then south, the ground running out ahead of them each way he turns, so that he never reaches the water.

title.zh: 天盖不住地，神派蜜蜂去偷听
title.en: The sky was too small, so God sent a bee

text.zh: 颂歌里的天出自海底那块金石头：鸽子把它像撒沙一样撒在水上，撒出明亮的天，再撒出太阳、月亮、晨星和满天小星。散文传说要收拾一个麻烦：地铺得太宽，天不够盖。听说刺猬知道办法，刺猬却不肯说，神便打发蜜蜂躲在一旁听——刺猬自言自语：把地捏拢就是了，捏出的褶子便是山和谷，天自然合得上。蜜蜂把话带了回去。刺猬咒它此后只配吃自己的粪，神把这句咒反了过来：蜜蜂的粪就是蜜。灵歌《鸽书》给的又是第三种答案：白光起于神的审判，红日出自主的脸，明月出自主的胸膛，繁星出自主的衣袍，暗夜出自主的心思，晨曦出自主的眼睛，狂风出自圣灵的气息。
text.en: In the carol the sky comes out of the golden stone from the sea floor: the doves sow it on the water as they sowed the sand, and it becomes the bright sky, and after it the bright sun, the clear moon, the morning star and the small stars. The prose legends have a problem to fix, because the earth has been spread too wide for the sky to cover. Word is that the hedgehog knows what to do, and the hedgehog will not tell God, so God sends a bee to hide nearby and listen, and the hedgehog mutters the answer to himself: squeeze the earth together, and the folds will be hills and valleys, and the sky will fit over it. The bee carries the answer back. The hedgehog curses her to eat nothing but her own dung, and God turns the curse the other way round, which is why a bee's dung is honey. The spiritual verse called the Golubinaya kniga gives a third answer entirely: the white light began from the judgment of God, the red sun came from the face of the Lord, the bright moon from his breast, the crowding stars from his robes, the dark nights from his thoughts, the morning dawns from his eyes, and the wild winds from the breath of the Holy Spirit.

title.zh: 狗拿一身皮毛换了看守之职
title.en: The dog took a fur coat as a bribe

text.zh: 神用泥捏出人形，还没吹进气，先搁在一边，留一条狗看着——那时的狗没有毛，光着身子。撒旦拿一件皮袄贿赂它，狗披上毛就放他过去了。撒旦在泥人身上啐唾沫、戳窟窿，人的百病都是这么来的。神回来没法重做，只好把人翻了个里外，把脏东西全翻进身体里头——人的病从此长在里面。狗留下了那身毛，也从此算不洁。至于人分几等，《鸽书》另有一说：沙皇出自亚当的头，公侯出自亚当的骨，庄稼汉出自亚当的膝。
text.en: God shapes a man out of clay and leaves the body lying while he goes to fetch its breath, setting a dog to watch it. The dog had no hair in those days and stood there bare. Satan buys the dog off with a fur coat, and the dog, now warm, lets him past. Satan spits on the clay body and jabs holes in it, and that is where sickness came from. God comes back and cannot start over, so he turns the man inside out, and all the filth ends up on the inside, which is why illness lives in a person's insides. The dog keeps the fur and has counted as unclean ever since. On where the ranks of people came from, the Golubinaya kniga answers separately: tsars came from Adam's head, princes and boyars from his bones, and the peasantry from his knee.

title.zh: 鲸鱼一翻身，白光世界就到头
title.en: When the whale-fish turns, the white world ends

text.zh: 定形之后的世界有一个中心：海中之海的布扬岛上压着白燃石阿拉特尔，《鸽书》称它为万石之母，一切治病的水都从它底下流出来。俄罗斯的咒语几乎都以「在海上，在大洋上，在布扬岛上，躺着白燃石阿拉特尔」开口，念咒的人要先站到那块石头上，才好开口求事。大地本身托在一条鲸鱼背上（有的抄本说三条）：鲸鱼一动，地就发抖，那是地震；等它彻底翻过身去，白光世界就到了头。
text.en: The finished world has a centre. On the island of Buyan, out in the sea-ocean, lies the white burning stone Alatyr, which the Golubinaya kniga names the mother of all stones, and from under it run the waters that cure. Russian charms open, almost as a rule, with the words: on the sea, on the ocean, on the island of Buyan lies the white burning stone Alatyr. The speaker plants himself on that stone before he asks for anything. The earth itself rides on the back of a whale-fish, three of them in some versions of the verse. When the fish shifts, the ground shakes, and that is an earthquake; when it turns over for good, the white world is finished.
